title: Cron & Repeat
description: Schedule recurring jobs with cron expressions

blocks.text: Hound supports recurring jobs via the repeat.pattern option. A cron expression makes a job self-schedule — after each successful execution, Hound re-enqueues it for the next occurrence automatically.
Set repeat.pattern on a handler registration or a JobDefinition to enable cron scheduling:
The first execution is scheduled when hound.start() is called. Subsequent executions are scheduled automatically after each completion.
You can also set a repeat pattern at emit time rather than at handler registration. This is useful for per-user or per-tenant scheduled jobs:
Always use a stable custom ID with per-emit repeat jobs. Without a fixed ID, each restart would enqueue an additional recurring job instance.
